task_context.actionable_question: Which demographic groups are scoring or engaging below the class average?
task_context.ai_prompt_hint: For each demographic group, compare mean avg_score [FE] and engagement_score [FE] to cohort average. Use colour intensity to show deviation. Avoid causal claims. Note: output is continuous score — distinct from A-G12 which uses categorical final_outcome.

key_db_fields: socioeconomic_band / gender / age_group / highest_education; avg_score [FE cross]

explanation.raw_text:
Summary: The analysis of demographic performance reveals significant disparities in average scores and engagement levels across different student groups. Notably, groups scoring below the cohort average exhibit lower engagement scores, indicating a potential area for targeted intervention.

Insights: High-Performing Group: Unknown: The 'Unknown' group demonstrates the highest average score of 81.21, significantly above the cohort average, indicating strong performance relative to peers. | Lowest Performing Group: 10-20%: The '10-20%' group has the lowest average score of 62.96, which is 7.48 points below the cohort average, highlighting a critical need for support. | Engagement Disparity Across Groups: Engagement scores are notably lower in groups with lower average scores, particularly in the '10-20%' group with an engagement score of 0.1312, indicating a correlation between engagement and performance.

Educational implications: Targeted interventions are necessary for lower-performing groups to enhance both scores and engagement.

Recommendations: high - Implement targeted support programs for the '10-20%' group. - This group is significantly underperforming and may benefit from additional resources and engagement strategies. | medium - Increase engagement activities for all groups, particularly those with lower scores. - Enhancing engagement may lead to improved academic performance across the board.

explanation.summary: The analysis of demographic performance reveals significant disparities in average scores and engagement levels across different student groups. Notably, groups scoring below the cohort average exhibit lower engagement scores, indicating a potential area for targeted intervention.

insights.title: High-Performing Group: Unknown
insights.description: The 'Unknown' group demonstrates the highest average score of 81.21, significantly above the cohort average, indicating strong performance relative to peers.

evidence.context: Cohort average is lower than this group's score.

insights.title: Lowest Performing Group: 10-20%
insights.description: The '10-20%' group has the lowest average score of 62.96, which is 7.48 points below the cohort average, highlighting a critical need for support.

evidence.context: This group's score is significantly lower than the cohort average.

insights.title: Engagement Disparity Across Groups
insights.description: Engagement scores are notably lower in groups with lower average scores, particularly in the '10-20%' group with an engagement score of 0.1312, indicating a correlation between engagement and performance.

evidence.context: This group's engagement score is among the lowest.

educational_implications: Targeted interventions are necessary for lower-performing groups to enhance both scores and engagement.

recommendations.action: Implement targeted support programs for the '10-20%' group.
recommendations.rationale: This group is significantly underperforming and may benefit from additional resources and engagement strategies.

recommendations.action: Increase engagement activities for all groups, particularly those with lower scores.
recommendations.rationale: Enhancing engagement may lead to improved academic performance across the board.

confidence.reason: The dataset is complete and provides a clear view of performance metrics across demographic groups.

required_core_outputs.description: For each demographic group, compare mean average score and engagement score with the cohort average.

evaluation_constraints.description: Quantify the magnitude of group deviation rather than relying on visual colour encoding alone.
Treat this as an equity-sensitive demographic group analysis.

evaluation_requirements.safety_fairness_note: Applicable because the task compares demographic groups and requires equity framing.
